title: Introduction to Amazon Simple Email Service SES

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.

tasks.title: Task 2: Create an Email Identity using SES

steps.text: Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to the Services menu at the top. Click on Amazon Simple Email Service in the Business Application section.
On the left side menu, Select Identities
Click on Create Identity.
Task 2: Create an Email Identity using SES
Enter the following details: Identity type: Select Email Address Email address: Enter your valid Email ID. Leave other options as default. Click on Create Identity.
Enter the following details:
Identity type: Select Email Address
Email address: Enter your valid Email ID.
Leave other options as default.
Click on Create Identity.
Task 2: Create an Email Identity using SES
You will get the below IAM error, just ignore that. You will not need this permission to complete the lab.
Task 2: Create an Email Identity using SES
Now you will be able to see that the Identity Status on the Email will be Verification Pending.
Task 2: Create an Email Identity using SES
AWS will send a confirmation email to the email that you provided in the above step.
Log in to your mailbox and you will be able to see a mail from Amazon Web Services.
Task 3: Verify the Email address
Once you click on the link, you will be routed to the success page.
Task 3: Verify the Email address
Now go to the SES Email Address page and refresh the page. You will be able to see the Verification status as Active.
Task 3: Verify the Email address
Let us send a test email to the same email address.
Click on Send Test Email.
Task 4: Send a Test Email

Make the following changes: Email Format: Select Formatted (keep it as default) From: Your email address will be present by default Scenario: Select Custom from the drop-down menu Custom recipient: Give the same email address (for testing purposes) Subject: Enter Sending a Test Email Body: Type any text as you wish Leave other options as default. Click on Send Test Email.
Make the following changes:
Email Format: Select Formatted (keep it as default)
From: Your email address will be present by default
Scenario: Select Custom from the drop-down menu
Custom recipient: Give the same email address (for testing purposes)
Subject: Enter Sending a Test Email
Body: Type any text as you wish
Leave other options as default.
Click on Send Test Email.
Task 4: Send a Test Email
Verify the mail received to your Inbox. If it is not present in your inbox, check in the spam.
Task 4: Send a Test Email

Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) is a cloud-based email sending and receiving service provided by Amazon Web Services (AWS). It is designed to help businesses and developers manage their email communications at scale. SES simplifies the process of sending transactional and marketing emails, such as notifications, alerts, newsletters, and promotional messages, while ensuring high deliverability rates and compliance with email industry standards.
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the Right side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample output :
Task 5: Validation Test
You have successfully logged in to the AWS Management console.
You have successfully created an SES Email address.
You have successfully created verified the Email address.
Sign out of the AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps click on End from the lab console.
